sources: USDA ARS. Storage Conditions for Plant Germplasm. https://www.ars.usda.gov/
NDSU Extension. Allowable Storage Time for Cereal Grains.
Ellis & Roberts (1980). Improved Equations for the Prediction of Seed Longevity. Annals of Botany 45(1):13-30.
Harrington (1972). Seed Storage and Longevity. In: Seed Biology Vol 3.

wheat.notes: Wheat seeds are highly storable under proper conditions. Drying to 10-12% MC and storing at -18C extends viability for decades. IBPGR standard for long-term: -18C, 5% MC.

rice.notes: Rice seed longevity benefits from controlled drying. Rupture force decreases significantly at higher moisture content.

corn.notes: Corn seeds have high mechanical damage resistance. Rupture force increases with moisture content, unlike most other grains.

soybean.notes: Soybeans are relatively short-lived due to high oil content. Store at <10C and <40% RH for best results. Most sensitive to storage conditions among common crops.

sunflower.notes: High oil content makes sunflower seeds susceptible to lipid oxidation. Requires lower moisture content than cereals for safe storage.

barley.notes: Similar storage behavior to wheat. For malting barley, germinability is critical and more sensitive to storage conditions than viability alone.

allowable_storage_days_cereals.description: Maximum days before significant quality loss for cereal grains at given MC and temperature. Source: NDSU Extension.

co2_monitoring_thresholds.description: CO2 levels in stored grain indicating spoilage. Source: World Grain, OPI Systems.

levels.interpretation: Stable grain mass, no biological activity detected
Onset of mold growth, investigate immediately
Active mold infection, mycotoxin risk developing
Severe mold/insect infestation, high mycotoxin risk

harrington_rules.rule_1: For each 1% decrease in seed moisture content, storage life approximately doubles (valid 5-14% MC range).
harrington_rules.rule_2: For each 5.6C (10F) decrease in storage temperature, storage life approximately doubles (valid 0-50C range).
harrington_rules.hundred_rule: Temperature (F) + Relative Humidity (%) should sum to less than 100 for safe seed storage.
